A LARGE MUDSLIDE OCCURRED ACROSS BOTH THE EASTBOUND AND WESTBOUND PORTIONS OF I-70. DEBRIS INCLUDED MUD, BRANCHES AND ROCKS. THE MUDSLIDE WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 2 TO 3 TIMES THE SIZE OF THE ONE THAT OCCURRED IN THE SAME AREA ON 26 JUNE 2021.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND EVIDENCE OF A MAJOR FLASH FLOODING EVENT IN THE WESTWATER DRAINAGE VALLEY. WATER WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 6 TO UP TO 12 FEET DEEP BASED ON EROSION OF THE ARROYO WALLS. NUMEROUS INSTANCES OF DRY ARROYO CROSSINGS WASHED OUT WITH MAJOR TRANSPORT OF SEDIMENT AND DEBRIS DOWNVALLEY.
MESONET STATION JAYC2 JAY, HOTCHKISS 3NNW.
1 TO 3 INCHES OF VERY SLICK MUD RAN ACROSS I70 AT MM128 OFF CINNAMON CREEK FROM THE SOUTH SIDE OF GLENWOOD CANYON. MUD ALSO RAN UNDERNEATH THE RAILROAD TRACKS NORTH OF I70 IN THIS SECTION.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 1 NW SHOSHONE. 1 TO 3 INCHES OF VERY SLICK MUD RAN ACROSS I70 AT MM128 OFF CINNAMON CREEK FROM THE SOUTH SIDE OF GLENWOOD CANYON. MUD ALSO RAN UNDERNEATH THE RAILROAD TRACKS NORTH OF I70 IN THIS SECTION. TIME OF REPORT IS WHEN IT WAS DISCOVERED, NOT WHEN IT OCCURRED.
ASOS STATION KCNY 16 NW MOAB.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 6 S GRAND CO ARPT. TIME WAS CORRECTED BASED ON REPORT FROM MOAB DISPATCH. UDOT REPORTED DEBRIS AT MM111 ON RT. 191. ROCKS AND OTHER DEBRIS CLOSED THE ROAD UNTIL IT COULD BE CLEARED. ROCKS WERE THE SIZE OF MICROWAVES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 16 NW MOAB. 5 TO 6 POWER LINES WERE BROKEN OFF JUST SOUTH OF THE CANYONLANDS AIRPORT NEAR BLUE HILLS RD. THIS WAS REPORTED TO MOAB DISPATCH AROUND 8:13 PM BUT LIKELY OCCURRED AN HOUR EARLIER WITH THE 70 MPH WIND GUST THAT OCCURRED FROM A SEVERE TSTORM.
UDOT REPORTED DEBRIS AT MM111 ON RT. 191. ROCKS AND OTHER DEBRIS CLOSED THE ROAD UNTIL IT COULD BE CLEARED.
